description: Hand-annotated ground truth for Rivera v. Harmon Construction Group. Each item is a real defect planted in the documents, with its origin. A finding 'catches' an item if its text (in the right category) contains every keyword of any one signal set (case-insensitive).

items.description: MSJ states the incident date as 'March 14, 2021'; the police report, medical records, and witness statement all say 'March 12, 2021'.
MSJ asserts as 'undisputed' that Rivera was NOT wearing required PPE; police report and witness statement say he WAS wearing a hard hat and harness.
MSJ claims Apex (not Harmon) controlled the work; the record shows Harmon's foreman Donner directed the crew to that section and dismissed a safety concern (retained-control exception to Privette).
MSJ quotes Privette for an absolute 'a hirer is never liable' rule; Privette is a REBUTTABLE presumption with recognized retained-control exceptions, so the rule is overstated.
MSJ says 'one year and 362 days' between incident (March 14, 2021 per MSJ) and filing (March 10, 2023) — a verifiable numeric claim worth checking.

Footnote 1 string-cites six cases (incl. out-of-state TX/FL) never discussed in the body; several appear to be unverifiable/likely fabricated and should be flagged as unverifiable rather than accepted.
